creative_brief: Hero image: Rachel in a working farm setting, candid and unpolished — not a studio portrait. Muted earth tones, natural light. Below the fold, a simple visual showing the short supply chain: farm icon → hub icon → family icon. No stock photography. The page should feel like a letter, not a billboard.
headline: I spent 15 years in food supply chains. I wouldn't feed my own kids what I saw.
subhead: That confession led to FarmThru — a farm-direct grocery store cutting out the middlemen so you can trace every egg, every cut, every leaf back to the actual farm. The equity crowdfunding campaign has a cap. Once it fills, this window closes.
hero_copy:
I watched perfectly good produce get trucked 2,400km across the country just so a middleman could slap a label on it. I watched farms get squeezed on price while families paid more at checkout. And I watched my own kids eat food I couldn't trace to a single paddock.

So we built something different. FarmThru partners with NSW farms — real names, real places — and gets their food to you through a hub-and-collect model. No unnecessary middlemen. No mystery supply chains. You order online, collect from Brookvale, and know exactly where it all came from.

sections.heading: The part that kept me up at night
sections.body:
Here's what the big chains don't advertise: by the time food reaches their shelves, it's passed through warehouses, distributors, repackagers, and cold storage facilities you'll never see. The farm name disappears. The story disappears. What's left is a barcode and a best-before date.

FarmThru works with around 15 to 20 partner farms and suppliers across NSW. We list them. You can look them up. That's not a marketing trick — it's the entire point. Farm to hub to you. The supply chain is short enough that you could drive it yourself on a Saturday.

sections.heading: No subscription. No lock-in. Just food you can trust.
sections.body:
Order when you want. Skip when you want. There's no membership fee, no weekly box you didn't ask for. FarmThru runs on-demand — you browse, you buy in bulk to save, you collect from our Brookvale hub Monday to Friday.

Unit 23, 10-18 Orchard Rd, Brookvale NSW 2100. That's not a warehouse pretending to be a store. It's a hub designed to keep costs low and bring the farm closer to your family.

No commitment required. No tricks. You shop on your terms.

sections.heading: The window to own a piece of this is closing
sections.body:
FarmThru is raising through equity crowdfunding on Birchal — and every campaign has a cap. Once it's filled, it's done. No extensions, no exceptions.

Minimum investment is $50. Maximum is $10,000 per investor. This isn't a donation. You'd own a piece of a farm-direct grocery model built to challenge how Australians buy food.

But investing in early-stage businesses carries real risk. You could lose some or all of your investment. There are no guaranteed returns. Read the offer document carefully before deciding.

If you want first access before the campaign opens, a $5 refundable deposit gets you VIP priority — first access to invest, priority updates, and an exclusive Q&A. Fully refundable if you change your mind.

sections.heading: Be part of it before the cap fills
sections.body:
This isn't about watching from the sidelines while someone else fixes the food system. It's about voting with your wallet — literally owning a piece of the change you want to see.

Join the VIP waitlist now. $5, fully refundable. You get priority access, exclusive updates, and a seat at the table before the crowd arrives.

Once the campaign cap is reached, this page becomes a thank-you note.

sections.heading: Compliance
sections.body: FarmThru's equity crowdfunding campaign is hosted on Birchal. Investing in early-stage businesses carries risk, including the potential loss of your entire investment. Returns are not guaranteed. This page is not financial advice. Please read the offer document and consider your own financial situation before making any investment decision. The $5 VIP deposit is fully refundable.
